models.name: GR-1
models.title: Unleashing Large-Scale Video Generative Pre-training for Visual Robot Manipulation
models.problem: Static VLA pretraining lacks temporal prediction for manipulation.
models.oneLine: A causal generative robot policy that jointly models language, observation history, future visual tokens, and actions.

insights.problem: Robot policies needed temporal structure beyond static image-language features.
insights.method: A MAE-style visual encoder and causal GPT sequence jointly predict future observations and actions.
insights.novelty: It made video generation a native policy-training objective rather than an auxiliary pretraining trick.
insights.limitation: By current standards the generation quality, scale, and deployment efficiency are limited.

models.name: GR-2
models.title: GR-2: A Generative Video-Language-Action Model with Web-Scale Knowledge for Robot Manipulation
models.problem: Scale GR-style future prediction into a more useful manipulation policy.
models.oneLine: A scaled video-language-action model using VQ-style future video tokens and a chunked cVAE action decoder.

insights.problem: GR-1 showed the direction but lacked broad real-world scale and strong future tokens.
insights.method: Frozen visual tokenization, web-scale video-language pretraining, future-video generation, and cVAE action chunks.
insights.novelty: It connected web-scale knowledge, future-token prediction, and low-data robot success more convincingly than GR-1.
insights.limitation: Action inference remains tied to a heavy generative sequence model.

metrics.runtimeNote: More deployable than GR-1, but still generative-sequence heavy.

models.name: LAPA
models.title: Latent Action Pretraining from Videos
models.problem: Unlabeled videos lack robot actions but contain useful transition structure.
models.oneLine: Learns discrete pseudo-actions from frame pairs so action-free video can supervise VLA pretraining.

insights.problem: Video data are abundant, but direct action labels are scarce and embodiment-specific.
insights.method: An inverse model compresses two-frame transitions into VQ latent action codes used as VLA pretraining targets.
insights.novelty: It made unlabeled video useful as action-like supervision without requiring a robot action stream.
insights.limitation: Two-frame discrete codes can be too coarse for fine manipulation and may not be executable.

models.name: VPP
models.title: Video Prediction Policy: A Generalist Robot Policy with Predictive Visual Representations
models.problem: Full video rollout is too expensive, but hidden predictive features may be action-useful.
models.oneLine: Uses a pretrained video predictor for one denoising step, aggregates predictive features, and feeds a diffusion action head.

insights.problem: Robot control needs future-aware state without paying for rendered video.
insights.method: A video model exposes one-step denoising features, Video Former compresses them, and a diffusion policy predicts actions.
insights.novelty: It established partial denoising plus feature aggregation as a practical video-to-action interface.
insights.limitation: The feature interface is sensitive to aggregation and layer choice.

metrics.runtimeNote: Single denoise step plus aggregation is the key shortcut.

models.name: UVA
models.title: Unified Video Action Model
models.problem: World modeling may help most as a training signal, not a deployed generator.
models.oneLine: Learns joint video-action latents with masked future prediction, then skips video generation at policy time.

insights.problem: Explicit online imagination adds latency to policies that only need actions.
insights.method: Encode history, actions, proprioception, and masked future VAE latents in one transformer; deploy a small action decoder.
insights.novelty: It cleanly separated video prediction during training from action-only runtime.
insights.limitation: Less interpretable because no explicit future is produced at deployment.

metrics.runtimeNote: No online video generation; compact conditional diffusion decoder.

models.name: UWM
models.title: Unified World Models: Coupling Video and Action Diffusion for Pretraining on Large Robotic Datasets
models.problem: Policy, inverse dynamics, forward dynamics, and joint prediction are usually separate models.
models.oneLine: A coupled diffusion transformer over future-observation latents and action chunks that can switch conditioning regimes.

insights.problem: Robotics operators are fragmented across separate predictors and policies.
insights.method: Noisy future observations and action chunks enter one DiT with independent modality timesteps.
insights.novelty: By setting modality noise levels, the same model becomes policy, forward model, inverse model, or joint predictor.
insights.limitation: Action inference can still pay for a future-observation branch, and pretraining scale is modest.

metrics.runtimeNote: DDIM acceleration helps, but future-observation coupling remains costly.

models.name: DreamGen
models.title: DreamGen: Unlocking Generalization in Robot Learning through Video World Models
models.problem: Low-coverage robot datasets limit downstream policy generalization.
models.oneLine: Uses LoRA-adapted image-to-video models as an offline synthetic trajectory engine, then relabels generated videos with actions.

insights.problem: Robot data are narrow and expensive; policies overfit to limited trajectories.
insights.method: Fine-tune video generators on robot data, roll out new videos from initial frames and language, then infer pseudo-actions.
insights.novelty: It treats the world model as an offline data multiplier rather than an online controller.
insights.limitation: High-quality generation and relabeling move compute offline instead of eliminating it.

models.name: FLARE
models.title: FLARE: Robot Learning with Implicit World Modeling
models.problem: Policies need future awareness without decoding future pixels.
models.oneLine: Adds future-latent slots to a diffusion policy and supervises them with compact action-aware future embeddings.

insights.problem: Explicit future generation is too slow and may over-emphasize appearance.
insights.method: Pretrain a future embedding model, then align hidden policy slots to the future embedding while denoising actions.
insights.novelty: The predicted future is a hidden condition inside action denoising, not a decoded video passed to a second policy.
insights.limitation: The lightweight runtime depends on a separate future-embedding target whose quality is doing much of the work.

models.name: CLAM
models.title: CLAM: Continuous Latent Action Models for Robot Learning from Unlabeled Demonstrations
models.problem: Discrete pseudo-actions are awkward for continuous robot control.
models.oneLine: Learns continuous latent actions through inverse dynamics, forward dynamics, and an action decoder.

insights.problem: VQ-style latent actions can be brittle and too coarse for smooth control.
insights.method: Jointly train latent inverse dynamics, latent forward dynamics, and a decoder into executable actions.
insights.novelty: Grounds latent actions with both predictive dynamics and action decodability.
insights.limitation: Still much stronger in narrower embodiment regimes than in broad cross-robot transfer.

models.name: VideoREPA
models.title: VideoREPA: Learning Physics for Video Generation through Relational Alignment with Foundation Models
models.problem: Video models can generate plausible clips while missing physical temporal structure.
models.oneLine: Aligns token-relation geometry of video models to stronger foundation-model representations.

insights.problem: Direct feature matching can damage video semantics and coherence.
insights.method: Use token relation distillation across spatial and temporal similarities rather than raw feature MSE.
insights.novelty: Injects physics-relevant structure while preserving the host model's representation geometry.
insights.limitation: Robotics relevance is indirect unless paired with an action interface.

models.name: UniVLA
models.title: UniVLA: Learning to Act Anywhere with Task-centric Latent Actions
models.problem: Cross-embodiment video is useful only if action abstractions are task-centric rather than robot-specific.
models.oneLine: Learns language-conditioned DINO-space latent actions from broad video, then uses a small embodiment decoder.

insights.problem: Heterogeneous videos should shape task dynamics without pretending to share low-level controls.
insights.method: Build a language-conditioned latent action codebook in DINO feature space and decode it per embodiment.
insights.novelty: Delays embodiment specificity to the decoder while broadening the shared latent prior.
insights.limitation: Embodiment-specific decoding is still necessary and can be the bottleneck.

models.name: Geometry Forcing
models.title: Geometry Forcing: Marrying Video Diffusion and 3D Representation for Consistent World Modeling
models.problem: RGB video models often lack viewpoint-consistent geometric structure.
models.oneLine: Regularizes video diffusion hidden states with geometry features using angular and scale-aware alignment.

insights.problem: Visually plausible videos can be geometrically inconsistent over long horizons.
insights.method: Align video hidden states to VGGT-style geometry and DINO-style semantics without naive MSE collapse.
insights.novelty: Uses pattern-preserving geometric alignment as an internal regularizer rather than a separate 3D renderer.
insights.limitation: It is a world-consistency method until coupled to an action decoder.

models.name: TriVLA
models.title: TriVLA: A Triple-System-Based Unified Vision-Language-Action Model with Episodic World Modeling for General Robot Control
models.problem: VLAs need both semantic grounding and predictive temporal context.
models.oneLine: Combines a VLM grounding system, a video diffusion feature system, and a flow-matching policy.

insights.problem: Language grounding alone is not enough for long-horizon or temporally sensitive control.
insights.method: A VLM extracts semantic tokens, a video model supplies predictive dynamics features, and a policy cross-attends to both.
insights.novelty: It packages predictive video features as an episodic world-model branch beside VLM grounding.
insights.limitation: The triple-system framing is broader than the actual mechanism; it is still a large VLM plus video diffusion plus policy stack.

models.name: VideoPolicy
models.title: VideoPolicy: Video Generators are Robot Policies
models.problem: Can hidden states of a video generator directly condition robot actions?
models.oneLine: Extracts intermediate SVD decoder features and feeds them to a diffusion-policy style action U-Net.

insights.problem: Full generated video is slow, but intermediate features may already encode task dynamics.
insights.method: Train video prediction on robot-like human videos, then condition a 1D action diffusion U-Net on selected hidden embeddings.
insights.novelty: Uses tool-shaped human demonstrations to narrow the morphology gap before action learning.
insights.limitation: Still inherits generator cost if too much of the video path is kept active.

metrics.runtimeNote: Feature tapping is cheaper than full rendering but still generator-dependent.

models.name: villa-X
models.title: villa-X: Enhancing Latent Action Modeling in Vision-Language-Action Models
models.problem: Latent action codes need proprioceptive and embodiment grounding to become reliable plans.
models.oneLine: Adds a proprioceptive forward-dynamics model and latent-plan expert to a VLA policy.

insights.problem: A visual latent can collapse into non-executable or embodiment-confounded codes.
insights.method: Train a latent action model with proprioceptive future prediction, then use a policy expert that predicts latent plans before actions.
insights.novelty: Tests whether latent actions predict future proprioception and action sequences, not only image change.
insights.limitation: The deployed policy is heavier, and some seen tasks can prefer a no-latent variant.

models.name: MoWM
models.title: MoWM: Mixture-of-World-Models for Embodied Planning via Latent-to-Pixel Feature Modulation
models.problem: Pixel and latent world models preserve different control-relevant information.
models.oneLine: Fuses pixel-space diffusion features with latent motion features to combine precision and abstraction.

insights.problem: Pixel futures keep detail but waste capacity; latent futures keep motion but can lose contact detail.
insights.method: Run pixel and latent world-model paths in parallel and fuse multiscale features for planning.
insights.novelty: Frames future state as a mixture of predictive abstractions rather than one representation.
insights.limitation: Its control value depends on whether the fused features align with action needs.

models.name: DUST

models.problem: A single noisy token stream can create conflict between visual generation and action quality.
models.oneLine: Uses separate vision and action diffusion streams with shared attention and asynchronous sampling.

insights.limitation: Adds inference knobs and produces strong but not revolutionary gains.

models.name: UD-VLA
models.title: Unified Diffusion VLA: Vision-Language-Action Model via Joint Discrete Denoising Diffusion Process
models.problem: Future image and action generation should be refined together rather than in separated passes.
models.oneLine: Quantizes images and actions into tokens, then jointly denoises future-image and action tokens.

insights.problem: Autoregressive future prediction followed by action decoding can loosen action-future coupling.
insights.method: Use text tokens, image VQ tokens, FAST action tokens, and masked discrete diffusion over future image/action blocks.
insights.novelty: Confidence-guided progressive reveal synchronizes action refinement with future visual hypotheses.
insights.limitation: A lot of the practical gain depends on tokenization and decoding engineering.

models.name: Motus

models.problem: Video objectives can overwhelm action quality unless action and world expertise are routed separately.
models.oneLine: A video-language-action expert mixture with action-dense and video-sparse scheduling through latent motion.

insights.problem: One monolithic generator may optimize prettier futures rather than better controls.
insights.method: Separate video, understanding, and action experts exchange information while actions pass through motion-like latents.
insights.novelty: Uses action-dense/video-sparse scheduling to keep control from being dominated by video prediction.
insights.limitation: Strong baseline but still heavy and later papers focus on reducing its inference cost.

models.name: Act2Goal
models.title: Act2Goal: From World Model To General Goal-conditioned Policy
models.problem: Goal images are underspecified static endpoints for long-horizon control.
models.oneLine: Imagines intermediate visual states toward a goal and controls over a multi-scale abstraction of that trajectory.

insights.problem: A single target image gives too little local guidance for manipulation.
insights.method: A goal-conditioned world model generates intermediate states, then multi-scale temporal hashing exposes near and far future cues.
insights.novelty: Uses decoded future as a goal bridge rather than open-ended continuation.
insights.limitation: Strong results depend materially on online hindsight relabeling and LoRA adaptation.

models.name: mimic-video
models.title: mimic-video: Video-Action Models for Generalizable Robot Control Beyond VLAs
models.problem: Large video generators contain useful dynamics but are too slow if fully decoded.
models.oneLine: Taps a middle activation of Cosmos Predict and conditions a reduced flow-matching action DiT on it.

insights.problem: Robotics needs the dynamics prior of a video generator without its full sampling cost.
insights.method: LoRA-adapt the video model, run partial denoising, and cross-attend an action DiT to a chosen hidden layer.
insights.novelty: Makes layer selection and partial denoising explicit in the action interface.
insights.limitation: Still depends on a large generator path and careful proprioception masking.

models.name: CLAP
models.title: CLAP: Contrastive Latent Action Pretraining for Learning Vision-Language-Action Models from Human Videos
models.problem: Human-video transitions include both executable robot actions and nuisance visual change.
models.oneLine: Aligns human-video transition latents to a frozen robot action codebook while separating nuisance dynamics.

insights.problem: Human video contains action-like signals but not all visible motion is robot-executable.
insights.method: Train a robot action VAE codebook, split video dynamics into action-relevant and nuisance branches, and contrastively align them.
insights.novelty: Treats human-to-robot latent action transfer as executable-codebook matching.
insights.limitation: Multi-stage complexity is high and some ablation gains are modest.

models.name: Cosmos Policy

models.problem: Actions, proprioception, and value-like state should enter the same geometry as video dynamics.
models.oneLine: Injects action/state/value information into video latent frames and pools action tokens back out after denoising.

insights.problem: Appending low-dimensional actions after video modeling can leave representation mismatch.
insights.method: Spatially replicate actions and proprioception into latent video frames and fine-tune a video model for control.
insights.novelty: Forces non-visual control variables into the video backbone's latent geometry.
insights.limitation: Heavy benchmark-specific post-training makes the reusable architectural contribution hard to isolate.

models.name: WoG

models.problem: Future images are expensive, but pure latent actions may discard useful task state.
models.oneLine: Predicts future condition vectors and future actions together so world modeling happens in policy condition space.

insights.problem: A controller needs future context, but not necessarily pixels.
insights.method: A future encoder extracts compact condition vectors from future observations; the VLA learns to self-predict them.
insights.novelty: Places world modeling in condition space, between explicit video rollout and latent-action-only supervision.
insights.limitation: If the condition representation drops contact-critical detail, the policy cannot recover it.

models.name: VLA-JEPA
models.title: VLA-JEPA: Enhancing Vision-Language-Action Model with Latent World Model
models.problem: Future representation learning should avoid leakage from future frames at inference.
models.oneLine: Predicts V-JEPA-style future latents from current observations, then finetunes an action head.

insights.problem: Pixel reconstruction is costly and future inputs can leak information during training.
insights.method: A target encoder sees future frames; a student path sees current frames and predicts future latent states.
insights.novelty: Provides a leakage-free semantic future target without rendering pixels.
insights.limitation: Not explicitly executable by itself; its ceiling depends on teacher latent quality.

models.name: FRAPPE
models.title: FRAPPE: Infusing World Modeling into Generalist Policies via Multiple Future Representation Alignment
models.problem: One future-representation teacher can impose blind spots on policy world modeling.
models.oneLine: Aligns a policy's predicted future representation to multiple frozen visual foundation teachers.

insights.problem: Future prediction targets should be broad enough to capture semantic and geometric consequences.
insights.method: Mid-training predicts future representations; post-training aligns parallel LoRA branches to multiple VFM teachers.
insights.novelty: Uses plural teacher alignment instead of one future embedding or decoded pixels.
insights.limitation: The staged multi-teacher recipe is complex and not a cheap one-knob method.

models.name: LDA-1B
models.title: LDA-1B: Scaling Latent Dynamics Action Model via Universal Embodied Data Ingestion
models.problem: Large heterogeneous embodied data should supervise different model roles instead of one imitation objective.
models.oneLine: A large latent-dynamics action model that routes data by role across policy, dynamics, inverse, and forecasting tasks.

insights.problem: Weak, action-free, and cross-embodiment data can hurt if treated as direct action labels.
insights.method: Predict DINO-space futures with decoupled visual/action streams and task-routed objectives over massive mixed data.
insights.novelty: Role-based universal embodied data ingestion is the main architectural contribution.
insights.limitation: Scale is a major confound: 1.6B parameters and very large curated data.

models.name: AdaWorldPolicy
models.title: World-Model-Driven Diffusion Policy with Online Adaptive Learning for Robotic Manipulation
models.problem: Offline world-action policies drift under OOD contact and visual conditions.
models.oneLine: Uses world, action, and force-prediction DiTs with online updates from imagination error and force error.

insights.problem: A frozen policy cannot correct systematic mismatch between imagined and observed futures.
insights.method: Alternate action generation and future imagination; update a small subset using visual and force prediction errors.
insights.novelty: Makes deployed prediction error a self-supervised adaptation signal, including wrist-force mismatch.
insights.limitation: Requires force sensing, runs slowly, and generated futures can blur in real scenes.

models.name: Say-Dream-Act
models.title: Say, Dream, and Act: Learning Video World Models for Instruction-Driven Robot Manipulation
models.problem: Dreamed video can help, but the policy must correct imagined errors.
models.oneLine: Distills a video generator for few-step rollout and trains the action policy to use but correct predicted futures.

insights.problem: Full video rollout is slow and imagined frames can be spatially wrong.
insights.method: Select and adapt a strong video generator, distill it for few-step prediction, and condition a policy on both real and dreamed observations.
insights.novelty: The action model is designed to correct predicted video rather than blindly trust it.
insights.limitation: Latency and long-horizon error accumulation remain explicit open problems.

metrics.runtimeNote: Few-step rollout maps speed-quality tradeoff.

models.name: CoWVLA
models.title: Chain of World: World Model Thinking in Latent Motion
models.problem: Dense future video is wasteful, but isolated latent actions lose temporal continuity.
models.oneLine: Keeps a continuous latent motion chain as the future variable for action generation.

insights.problem: Future reasoning should preserve temporal motion structure without rendering every frame.
insights.method: Factorize video into structure and motion latents, infer a latent motion chain, and align it with sparse keyframes/actions.
insights.novelty: Uses temporally extended motion chains rather than one-step latent action codes.
insights.limitation: Depends heavily on clean structure/motion disentanglement from the video VAE.

models.name: Fast-WAM
models.title: Fast-WAM: Do World Action Models Need Test-time Future Imagination?
models.problem: The field conflates video co-training with test-time future generation.
models.oneLine: Trains with joint video/action objectives but removes explicit future imagination during deployment.

insights.problem: It is unclear whether online future generation or training-time predictive pressure drives WAM gains.
insights.method: Compare matched variants and deploy a single-pass action-only decoder with cached visual context.
insights.novelty: Directly isolates co-training from runtime imagination as separate factors.
insights.limitation: It does not prove explicit imagination is useless for all harder planning regimes.

models.name: S-VAM
models.title: S-VAM: Shortcut Video-Action Model by Self-Distilling Geometric and Semantic Foresight
models.problem: Multi-step foresight is useful but too slow for reactive control.
models.oneLine: Self-distills a base video-action model's semantic and geometric multi-step foresight into a one-step shortcut.

insights.problem: One-step features are noisy, while multi-step rollout is costly.
insights.method: Train a shortcut denoiser to imitate the base model's own multi-step semantic/geometric foresight.
insights.novelty: Distills the expensive controller-relevant representation rather than raw ground-truth future frames.
insights.limitation: If the teacher rollout is wrong, the shortcut inherits the error.

models.name: SimDist
models.title: Simulation Distillation: Pretraining World Models in Simulation for Rapid Real-World Adaptation
models.problem: Sim-to-real adaptation can destroy useful representation geometry if too much is unfrozen.
models.oneLine: Distills simulator rewards, values, and dynamics into a world model, then adapts only dynamics mismatch.

insights.problem: Adapting all components during transfer can cause representation drift and forgetting.
insights.method: Pretrain with simulator rewards/values and suboptimal policies, then freeze semantics, reward, value, and planner interfaces.
insights.novelty: Separates stable high-level representation geometry from low-level dynamics correction.
insights.limitation: Assumes privileged simulator rewards, saved policies, and a useful simulator.

models.name: VAMPO
models.title: VAMPO: Policy Optimization for Improving Visual Dynamics in Video Action Models
models.problem: Standard video losses do not directly optimize control-relevant future dynamics.
models.oneLine: Post-trains a video action model's denoising path with reward-weighted optimization over future latents.

insights.problem: Plausible futures are not necessarily the futures that support good actions.
insights.method: Treat denoising as a sequential policy and optimize with GRPO-style rewards in latent future space.
insights.novelty: Localizes stochastic exploration to early denoising while keeping later steps deterministic.
insights.limitation: Training stability is delicate and rewards still depend on expert future latents.

metrics.runtimeNote: Improves policy quality more than raw speed.

models.name: EVA
models.title: EVA: Aligning Video World Models with Executable Robot Actions via Inverse Dynamics Rewards
models.problem: A video future can look plausible while requiring jerky or infeasible robot actions.
models.oneLine: Uses a frozen inverse-dynamics model as a reward to align generated futures with executable action sequences.

insights.problem: Standard video metrics miss action smoothness, limits, and executability.
insights.method: Decode candidate futures through a frozen IDM and reward smooth, bounded, executable action sequences.
insights.novelty: Makes inverse dynamics quality part of video world-model alignment.
insights.limitation: The reward captures kinematics but not force closure, friction, slip, or contact stability.

models.name: VTAM
models.title: VTAM: Video-Tactile-Action Models for Complex Physical Interaction Beyond VLAs
models.problem: RGB-only world models miss contact and force variables critical for manipulation.
models.oneLine: Injects tactile streams into a shared video-latent world model and predicts virtual force alongside actions.

insights.problem: Late tactile fusion can fail because touch never shapes the world model state.
insights.method: Encode multiview RGB and tactile streams with intra/cross-view attention in a shared predictive latent.
insights.novelty: Uses virtual-force prediction as an anti-collapse regularizer for contact-aware dynamics.
insights.limitation: Evidence is narrow, slow, and based on virtual deformation-derived force rather than direct force-torque measurement.

models.name: GigaWorld-Policy
models.title: GigaWorld-Policy: An Efficient Action-Centered World-Action Model
models.problem: World-action training is useful, but deployed action inference should not depend on generated future video.
models.oneLine: Keeps future video as training-time structure while dropping future visual tokens from the deployed action path.

insights.problem: Imagine-then-act systems can be too slow for practical deployment.
insights.method: Train action prediction and future video generation together with causal masking that prevents future leakage into actions.
insights.novelty: Action-centered WAM design uses future video to sharpen representation without runtime visual generation.
insights.limitation: Efficiency gain is mainly deployment-time; training still requires a large robot-video stack.

models.name: AIM
models.title: AIM: Intent-Aware Unified World Action Modeling with Spatial Value Maps
models.problem: Future RGB does not explicitly tell the policy where interaction should happen.
models.oneLine: Routes future information to actions through aligned spatial value maps inside a unified WAM.

insights.problem: A policy may infer the wrong contact location from plausible future video.
insights.method: Jointly predict future RGB and spatial value maps, then restrict action access through intent-causal attention.
insights.novelty: Turns future prediction into an explicit where-to-act bottleneck.
insights.limitation: Value maps rely on privileged simulation supervision and are not generic weak labels.

models.name: WAV
models.title: World-Value-Action Model: Implicit Planning for Vision-Language-Action Systems
models.problem: Long-horizon tasks need utility-aware future reasoning rather than direct one-shot action regression.
models.oneLine: Learns latent future trajectories, scores them with a value model, and shapes action inference toward useful futures.

insights.problem: Behavior cloning can miss long-horizon utility structure.
insights.method: Predict latent future trajectories, score them with a trajectory-value function, and infer actions under high-value feasible futures.
insights.novelty: Moves planning into a learned latent future manifold rather than explicit MPC over actions.
insights.limitation: Still a heavy finetuning setup, and planning gains are strongest on hard long-horizon suites.

models.name: DexWorldModel

models.oneLine: Predicts DINO latent futures with constant-memory TTT state and speculative asynchronous inference.

models.name: X-WAM
models.title: Unified 4D World Action Modeling from Video Priors with Asynchronous Denoising
models.problem: 2D RGB futures lack geometry needed for contact and multiview manipulation.
models.oneLine: Extends unified WAMs to multiview RGB-D futures and denoises action/world branches asynchronously.

insights.problem: Future RGB alone cannot reliably bind action to geometry and camera calibration.
insights.method: Add a depth branch to a video diffusion backbone and train action/world timesteps for asynchronous denoising.
insights.novelty: Combines multiview RGB-D future prediction with different action and world denoising speeds.
insights.limitation: The 4D state is multiview RGB-D forecasting, not a persistent explicit 3D memory, and the recipe is expensive.

models.name: Vidar
models.title: Vidar: Embodied Video Diffusion Model for Generalist Manipulation
models.problem: New robot embodiments should not need large homogeneous action-labeled datasets before video priors become useful.
models.oneLine: A pretrained video diffusion prior generates embodied rollouts, then a masked inverse dynamics model converts action-relevant pixels into controls.

insights.problem: End-to-end pixel-to-action policies are brittle under embodiment, viewpoint, and background shift when target demonstrations are scarce.
insights.method: Continue-pretrain Wan/Vidu-style video diffusion on 750K multi-view robot episodes in a unified observation space, adapt on about 20 minutes of target videos, and decode actions with MIDM.
insights.novelty: Separates a large embodiment-aware video prior from a lightweight sparse-mask inverse dynamics adapter trained without dense segmentation labels.
insights.limitation: Open-loop video generation is very slow: one 60-frame rollout is reported at about 25 seconds on 8x80GB GPUs, with test-time reranking adding more compute.

metrics.runtimeNote: Open-loop 60-frame video generation is reported at about 25 seconds on 8x80GB GPUs; K=3 rollouts are reranked.

models.name: Genie Envisioner
models.title: Genie Envisioner: A Unified World Foundation Platform for Robotic Manipulation
models.problem: Robot world models, action policies, and simulators are usually trained and evaluated as separate systems.
models.oneLine: AgiBot GE-Base learns multi-view robotic video dynamics, while GE-Act mirrors the video DiT with a lightweight action DiT that cross-attends to visual latents.

insights.problem: Robotic manipulation needs one foundation platform for policy learning, evaluation, and simulation rather than disconnected models.
insights.method: Train GE-Base as a multi-view instruction-conditioned video DiT on AgiBot-World, then attach GE-Act, a 160M action DiT decoder conditioned on cached visual latents.
insights.novelty: Uses slow-fast asynchronous inference: sparse visual latent generation at 5 Hz feeds dense 30 Hz action denoising without decoding full video at deployment.
insights.limitation: The core pretraining corpus is AgiBot-World-Beta, so platform coverage and external reproducibility depend on dataset release and transfer quality.

models.name: XR-1
models.title: XR-1: Towards Versatile Vision-Language-Action Models via Learning Unified Vision-Motion Representations
models.problem: Cross-embodiment VLA training needs a representation that connects visual dynamics with robot motion instead of treating them as separate signals.
models.oneLine: A dual-branch VQ-VAE learns Unified Vision-Motion Codes, then a VLM predicts those codes as auxiliary structure while training an action head.

insights.problem: VLAs struggle to reuse human/actionless visual dynamics and robot motion data across embodiments.
insights.method: Learn a shared discrete codebook from future-frame visual dynamics and proprioceptive action-motion sequences, then inject learnable UVMC tokens into VLM pretraining.
insights.novelty: Makes the latent code explicitly bimodal: visual future changes and low-level robot motion quantize into one code space with alignment regularization.
insights.limitation: The system is large and data-heavy; attribution between UVMC learning, dataset scale, and downstream post-training is difficult.

models.name: ViPRA
models.title: ViPRA: Video Prediction for Robot Actions
models.problem: Actionless human and robot videos contain dynamics but not executable robot labels.
models.oneLine: ViPRA learns discrete motion-centric latent actions from videos, pretrains a video-language model to predict future visual tokens and latent actions, then maps latents to continuous chunks with flow matching.

insights.problem: Large video corpora are useful for robot control only if the model can extract action-like dynamics without action annotations.
insights.method: Train a quantized latent action model with inverse and forward dynamics, predict future visual tokens plus latent action chunks with LWM, then fine-tune a continuous flow decoder on small robot demos.
insights.novelty: Combines future-state prediction and multi-step latent action chunking rather than treating latent actions as one-step autoregressive policy labels.
insights.limitation: The latent action pretraining corpus is still limited, and real-world finetuning is required for smooth continuous control.

metrics.runtimeNote: Reports effective control up to 22 Hz via chunked action decoding; flow decoder uses 10 Euler steps.

models.name: LingBot-VA
models.title: Causal World Modeling for Robot Control
models.problem: Chunked or bidirectional WAMs lose causality, long-context memory, or reactivity during closed-loop control.
models.oneLine: An autoregressive dual-stream diffusion transformer interleaves sparse video tokens and high-frequency action tokens, with MoT fusion, KV cache, partial denoising, and asynchronous execution.

insights.problem: Robot policies need persistent memory and real feedback rather than open-loop chunk rollouts.
insights.method: Initialize a Wan2.2-5B video stream, add a narrower action stream, fuse them through MoT attention, and train causal video/action flow matching over interleaved tokens.
insights.novelty: Combines causal KV-cache rollout, partial denoising for video latents, and FDM-grounded asynchronous inference to avoid stale hallucinated context.
insights.limitation: The architecture relies on a large 5.3B model and substantial mixed robot data; web-scale pretraining details are less transparent than method mechanics.

metrics.runtimeNote: Uses 3 video Euler steps to partial denoising plus 10 action steps, KV cache, and async execution.

models.name: DreamZero
models.title: World Action Models are Zero-shot Policies
models.problem: WAMs need zero-shot generalization, cross-embodiment transfer, and real-time control without separating video and action too loosely.
models.oneLine: A 14B autoregressive video diffusion transformer jointly denoises future video latents and action tokens in one sequence, then uses cache, async execution, and Flash distillation for real-time control.

insights.problem: Separate video prediction plus IDM can misalign futures and actions, while bidirectional chunk models accumulate errors in closed-loop control.
insights.method: Fine-tune Wan2.1-I2V with action tokens in the same autoregressive chunk sequence as video latents, using teacher-forced flow matching and closed-loop KV cache replacement.
insights.novelty: Treats WAM pretraining itself as a zero-shot policy objective over diverse non-repetitive robot video/action data.
insights.limitation: The strongest model is very large and system-heavy; many speedups depend on hardware, caching, quantization, and a Flash variant.

models.name: Rhoda DVA
models.title: Causal Video Models Are Data-Efficient Robot Policy Learners
models.problem: Direct robot policies need the scale and long-context memory of video models without waiting for massive robot-action datasets.
models.oneLine: Rhoda DVA uses a causal video model as the decision-maker, then translates generated futures to real-time actions through an inverse dynamics model and leapfrog execution.

insights.problem: VLAs have short visual context and require much more robot data than video models can learn from at web scale.
insights.method: Autoregressively generate video from long context, translate predicted video to actions with an IDM, and overlap generation with execution in a closed-loop leapfrog pipeline.
insights.novelty: Frames robot control as direct causal video generation with long-context visual memory and in-context human-demo conditioning.
insights.limitation: The public source is a research page with qualitative videos and no conventional benchmark table or detailed architecture paper.

models.name: DiT4DiT
models.title: DiT4DiT: Jointly Modeling Video Dynamics and Actions for Generalizable Robot Control
models.problem: Action policies should exploit video diffusion dynamics directly, not only decoded future frames or frozen hidden features.
models.oneLine: A cascaded video DiT and action DiT are jointly trained with dual flow matching; the action DiT cross-attends to intermediate denoising features at a fixed feature timestep.

insights.problem: Separate video generation and action training can underuse the video model and produce unstable action features.
insights.method: Initialize video DiT from Cosmos-Predict2.5, hook intermediate hidden states at tau_f, and train a GR00T-style action DiT jointly with video flow loss.
insights.novelty: Introduces a tri-timestep scheme separating video denoising timestep, feature-extraction timestep, and action denoising timestep.
insights.limitation: Still depends on a heavy video backbone and reaches about 6 Hz in the reported A100 setup; view occlusion remains a concern in egocentric setups.

metrics.runtimeNote: Action path uses a single deterministic video feature extraction pass plus action denoising; reported around 6 Hz on A100.

models.name: Being-H0.7
models.title: Being-H0.7: A Latent World-Action Model from Egocentric Videos
models.problem: VLAs learn shortcut action mappings, while pixel-space world models waste compute rendering task-irrelevant visual detail.
models.oneLine: A latent world-action model that reasons about the future in a latent space via aligned prior/posterior branches, deploying with no visual rollout.

insights.problem: Sparse action supervision encourages shortcuts rather than dynamics; pixel rollouts add overhead and model irrelevant detail.
insights.method: A deployable prior branch infers latent future states from current context; a training-only posterior branch reads future-observation embeddings, and the two are aligned in latent reasoning space.
insights.novelty: Keeps predictive benefit of world models while eliminating pixel rollout, discarding the posterior branch entirely at inference.
insights.limitation: Latent future awareness is only as good as the posterior embeddings used during training; no inspectable future is produced.

metrics.runtimeNote: Latent prior branch, no visual rollout; latency-aware async chunking ~3-4 ms/step.

models.name: VERA
models.title: Turning Video Models into Generalist Robot Policies
models.problem: Robot foundation models finetune a video model per embodiment, which is expensive and destroys cross-embodiment reuse.
models.oneLine: Decouples an embodiment-agnostic action-free video planner from a Jacobian inverse-dynamics model for zero-shot cross-embodiment control.

insights.problem: Jointly finetuning video prediction and action per embodiment is costly and non-transferable.
insights.method: Keep the action-free video generative model unchanged as a visual planner; pair it with an embodiment-specific Jacobian inverse-dynamics model (J-IDM) trained on self-play.
insights.novelty: Video model is reused across embodiments without retraining; embodiment specificity is isolated in a small Jacobian IDM.
insights.limitation: Closed-loop video rollout with a 14B backbone is heavy; performance depends on video plan quality.

metrics.runtimeNote: Closed-loop video rollout planner (up to 14B) plus Jacobian IDM translation.

models.name: HarmoWAM
models.title: HarmoWAM: Harmonizing Generalizable and Precise Manipulation via Adaptive World Action Models
models.problem: Imagine-then-Execute WAMs generalize but lack precision; Joint-Modeling WAMs are precise but confined to their training distribution.
models.oneLine: A world model that harmonizes a predictive expert and a reactive expert through process-adaptive gating for both generalizable transit and precise contact.

insights.problem: The two dominant WAM paradigms trade off generalization against precision.
insights.method: A shared world model drives a reactive expert (direct action from predicted visuals) for exploration and a predictive expert (latent dynamics) for precise interaction, with a process-adaptive gate deciding when/where to switch.
insights.novelty: Unifies predictive and reactive control through automatic gating rather than committing to a single paradigm.
insights.limitation: Evaluated only on real-world tasks; no standard simulation benchmark scores reported.

metrics.runtimeNote: 48 Hz action generation via reactive expert; world model uses 5 denoising steps.

models.name: Pelican-Unify 1.0
models.title: Pelican-Unify 1.0: A Unified Embodied Intelligence Model for Understanding, Reasoning, Imagination and Action
models.problem: Embodied stacks fragment understanding, reasoning, world modeling, and action into separate specialists that never jointly optimize.
models.oneLine: A single foundation model where one VLM handles understanding and reasoning and one diffusion transformer jointly imagines future video and actions.

insights.problem: Modular assembly of VLM + policy + world model prevents consequence-aware action, steerable imagination, and grounded reasoning.
insights.method: One VLM (Qwen3-VL) encodes all modalities and autoregressively produces interleaved video/action chain-of-thought into a loop state z; a single Wan2.2 DiT with two lightweight heads denoises future video and action from z.
insights.novelty: Structural unification via shared embedders, mutual conditioning, and co-evolving gradients rather than modular hand-off.
insights.limitation: Failures concentrate on long-horizon geometry-sensitive tasks; real-world evaluation limited to two platforms.

metrics.runtimeNote: Autoregressive CoT then diffusion imagination of joint video+action from a shared loop state.

models.name: WALL-WM
models.title: WALL-WM: Carving World Action Modeling at the Event Joints
models.problem: Fixed-length action chunks misalign language, vision, and control timescales, so WAMs learn short-horizon correlations instead of composable world models.
models.oneLine: Event-grounded VLA pretraining that makes semantically coherent manipulation events the atomic unit of video-action learning.

insights.problem: Chunk-centric optimization fragments semantic goals, continuous dynamics, and control into misaligned windows.
insights.method: A frozen Wan video tower is layer-coupled to an Action DiT with event-grounded captions; Camera RoPE and sight-cone/tube masking give geometry-aware multi-view learning, and a Staircase parallel decoder relays latent chain-of-thought.
insights.novelty: Shifts pretraining from fixed chunks to semantically coherent events, with layer-wise one-directional video->action coupling and parallel latent CoT decoding.
insights.limitation: Fixed-length codec truncates events beyond ~65 latent frames; asymmetric schedule may loosen video-action co-denoising.

metrics.runtimeNote: Event-length video rollout; Staircase parallel latent CoT skips the autoregressive token bottleneck.

models.name: Next Forcing
models.title: Next Forcing: Causal World Modeling with Multi-Chunk Prediction
models.problem: Teacher-forced video world models exploit appearance copying between adjacent chunks (myopic supervision), stalling dynamics learning at high frame rates.
models.oneLine: Augments autoregressive video-action world models with multi-chunk prediction heads that supervise several future horizons at once.

insights.problem: Adjacent chunks look similar, so models shortcut dynamics by copying appearance, especially at 50 fps.
insights.method: A Wan2.2 backbone with a video/action mixture-of-transformers gains three lightweight MCP modules predicting next1/next2/next3 chunks, fused from four intermediate layers with causal chaining across depths.
insights.novelty: Extends multi-token prediction from language to continuous denoised video latents with causal chaining, discardable for zero overhead or reused for 2x parallel generation.
insights.limitation: MCP modules add training cost that is not precisely quantified.

metrics.runtimeNote: Autoregressive causal video; MCP heads discardable for zero-overhead or reused for 2x parallel generation.

models.name: Feedback World Model
models.title: Feedback World Model Enables Precise Guidance of Diffusion Policy
models.problem: World models are treated as static predictors at inference, so their errors accumulate under distribution shift and misguide the policy.
models.oneLine: Maintains a lightweight online feedback state that corrects world-model predictions from real observations to guide a diffusion policy without retraining.

insights.problem: Static world-model predictions become unreliable out of distribution at deployment.
insights.method: A latent-observer feedback state z-bar is updated online from the discrepancy e = z - z-bar with gain L, and the corrected predictions guide diffusion denoising in the final low-noise steps.
insights.novelty: Online feedback correction without parameter updates, plus action-aware guidance weighting via counterfactual variance.
insights.limitation: Guidance during denoising adds inference latency over an unguided base policy.

metrics.runtimeNote: Online feedback correction; guidance applied only in the final low-noise denoising steps adds latency.

models.name: OmniHumanoid
models.title: OmniHumanoid: Streaming Cross-Embodiment Video Generation with Paired-Free Adaptation
models.problem: Cross-embodiment motion transfer entangles transferable dynamics with embodiment-specific appearance and needs paired data per target robot.
models.oneLine: A streaming cross-embodiment video generator that factorizes shared motion from embodiment-specific appearance for paired-free data synthesis.

insights.problem: Motion dynamics transfer across embodiments but appearance and morphology do not, and prior methods require paired data per embodiment.
insights.method: A shared motion-transfer model learned from motion-aligned paired videos plus lightweight embodiment-specific adapters, with a branch-isolated attention design separating motion conditioning from embodiment modulation.
insights.novelty: Paired-free adaptation to unseen embodiments by isolating motion from embodiment via branch attention.
insights.limitation: It is an offline data-generation video model, not an online policy; abstract-level detail only.

metrics.runtimeNote: Offline streaming cross-embodiment video generation used as a data engine, not an online controller.

models.name: MoLA
models.title: From Imagined Futures to Executable Actions: Mixture of Latent Actions for Robot Manipulation
models.problem: Video generators optimize perceptual realism, not manipulation-relevant structure, so imagined futures are hard to turn into actions.
models.oneLine: Bridges video imagination and control with a mixture of modality-aware inverse-dynamics models that decode predicted futures into structured latent actions.

insights.problem: Visual predictions are not inherently action-oriented; conditioning policies on raw frames is brittle.
insights.method: An SVD imagination model plus a mixture of three pretrained IDMs specialized for semantic (SAM2), depth (Depth Anything v2), and flow (CoTracker3) modalities produce complementary latent actions consumed by a flow-matching DiT action head.
insights.novelty: First structured latent-action interface coupling video generation to policy via a modality-aware IDM mixture that beats unified alternatives.
insights.limitation: Depends on video-generation quality and a frozen MoIDM that cannot adapt to an evolving policy.

metrics.runtimeNote: Single-denoising-step imagination; mixture of IDMs decode latent actions for a flow-matching head.

models.name: MaskWAM
models.title: MaskWAM: Unifying Mask Prompting and Prediction for World-Action Models
models.problem: Text inputs are referentially ambiguous in clutter and unstructured RGB predictions lack semantic grounding, creating a spatial bottleneck in WAMs.
models.oneLine: An object-centric WAM that jointly predicts future RGB, task-relevant masks, and actions while accepting optional visual mask prompts.

insights.problem: WAMs suffer spatial bottlenecks from ambiguous language and semantically ungrounded RGB prediction.
insights.method: RGB and rendered mask frames are encoded by the same Wan 2.2 VAE and concatenated channel-wise; a mixture-of-transformers with block-wise causal attention jointly denoises RGB, mask, and action with decoupled noise schedules.
insights.novelty: First to unify mask-based visual prompting with future mask prediction as joint training objectives inside a WAM.
insights.limitation: Relies on mask supervision in training and SAM-3 segmentation at deployment; reliable mask extraction in clutter is hard.

models.name: ABot-M0.5
models.title: ABot-M0.5: Unified Mobility-and-Manipulation World Action Model
models.problem: VLAs are reactive and lack world modeling, while existing WAMs are poorly aligned with the structure of mobile manipulation.
models.oneLine: A unified mobility-and-manipulation WAM that bridges video and control with frame-level latent actions and dual-level action disentanglement.

insights.problem: Mobile manipulation faces coarse video prediction vs fine control, entangled heterogeneous actions, and train-test inverse-dynamics gaps.
insights.method: A Wan2.2 5B video diffusion backbone uses frame-level latent actions plus a dual-level mixture-of-transformers that disentangles modality and separates mobility and manipulation, trained with cascade conditional flow matching and Dream Forcing.
insights.novelty: Intermediate embodiment-agnostic latent actions, dual-level action decoupling, and Dream Forcing for train-test alignment.
insights.limitation: Real-world transfer and scalability beyond proof-of-concept remain understudied.

models.name: LaWAM
models.title: LaWAM: Latent World Action Models for Efficient Dynamics-Aware Robot Policies
models.problem: Pixel-level WAMs are expensive, slow at inference, and do not expose action-relevant dynamics to the action generator.
models.oneLine: Predicts compact latent visual subgoals instead of pixels by repurposing a latent-action-model decoder as a non-iterative world model.

insights.problem: Pixel synthesis is costly and its dynamics are not action-relevant for downstream control.
insights.method: A Qwen3-VL/DINOv3 policy predicts latent actions; the LaWM decoder converts them to a latent subgoal in one forward pass, and an Alternate-DiT action expert generates the chunk with knowledge insulation.
insights.novelty: Repurposes the forward decoder of a latent-action model as an explicit, non-iterative world-model interface over latent subgoals.
insights.limitation: Most effective with stable cameras; struggles with egocentric shake or large viewpoint change.

metrics.runtimeNote: Non-iterative latent subgoal decode (~187 ms/chunk, 10 steps); up to 24x lower latency than pixel WAMs.

models.name: AHA-WAM
models.title: AHA-WAM: Asynchronous Horizon-Adaptive World-Action Modeling with Observation-Guided Context Routing
models.problem: WAMs couple video prediction and action at the same temporal resolution, forcing the video branch to model redundant short-horizon variation.
models.oneLine: Decouples slow long-horizon video planning from fast action execution via an asynchronous dual-DiT with observation-guided context routing.

insights.problem: Same-resolution coupling wastes the video branch on short-horizon frame variation and slows control.
insights.method: A Wan2.2-5B video DiT plans long-horizon while a compact action DiT runs high-frequency closed-loop; Observation-Guided Video-Context Routing with 32 queries and a rolling K/V memory adapt cached context without recomputing the video DiT.
insights.novelty: Asynchronous horizon-decoupled formulation with observation-conditioned context routing and horizon-adaptive offset training.
insights.limitation: Temporal hyperparameters lack principled tuning; evaluated only on short-to-medium horizons.

models.name: tau0-WM
models.title: tau0-WM: A Unified Video-Action World Model for Robotic Manipulation
models.problem: Robots need to generate executable actions while anticipating and evaluating their consequences before acting, across heterogeneous data.
models.oneLine: A unified video-action world model that proposes actions, then evaluates and revises them at test time using its own learned video simulator.

insights.problem: Data sources differ in embodiment, viewpoint, and action fidelity; video prediction is usually only an auxiliary loss.
insights.method: A Wan 5B video DiT plus a 0.5B action DiT are jointly flow-matched; at test time re-denoising consistency scoring selects among sampled actions, an action-conditioned video simulator evaluates rollouts, and low-quality actions are rectified by re-querying the policy.
insights.novelty: Uses video prediction as a deployment-time mechanism for action selection and rectification, not just training signal.
insights.limitation: Single-embodiment scope, no tactile feedback, and the test-time fallback adds latency.

metrics.runtimeNote: Test-time proposal-evaluation-revision; fallback video rollout on unreliable actions on a 5.5B model.

models.name: RLA-WM
models.title: Learning Visual Feature-Based World Models via Residual Latent Action
models.problem: Direct-regression feature world models are blurry and generative models over full DINO token space are computationally prohibitive.
models.oneLine: A visual-feature world model that predicts future DINO features via compact residual latent actions with flow matching.

insights.problem: Predicting future visual features accurately while staying efficient is hard in high-dimensional token space.
insights.method: An autoencoder compresses DINO residuals into a 2048-dim residual latent action; a flow-matching network predicts velocity in that compact space and a decoder reconstructs future DINO tokens.
insights.novelty: First to learn latent actions from DINO residuals and run flow matching in a compact feature space, enabling RL entirely within an offline world model.
insights.limitation: Single-frame-pair training limits occlusion handling; evaluated on small-scale datasets only.

metrics.runtimeNote: Compact DINO-residual latent world model; 30 flow-matching steps in feature space, no pixel decode.

accuracy.note: Excluded from Metrics accuracy because no requested target benchmark result was found.

evidence.text: Latent action learning achieves 35.6% average success versus 27.2% for BC-ResNet, and world-model RL improves success by 6.1% over behavior cloning.

accuracy.ignoredForAccuracyFilters: CALVIN, MetaWorld, Bridge, DROID, Language Table, Open X, and generic real-robot mentions are excluded from benchmark-specific accuracy filters, but can still support generalization evidence.
accuracy.note: Metrics accuracy includes only papers with an agent-audited hard target result. The estimated view is anchored to SimplerEnv and RoboCasa because they are less saturated; RobotWin is retained but compressed and downweighted. LIBERO results are excluded from Metrics benchmarks.

computeCost.pretraining: GPU-hours for training from scratch, broad pretraining, or generalist policy pretraining; reported GPU-hours are used where present, otherwise estimated from computeScale, parameter count, and training scope.
computeCost.finetuning: GPU-hours normalized to 300 one-minute task episodes, i.e. 5h task data; reported fine-tune GPU-hours are scaled when context gives task count, otherwise estimated from computeScale.

inferenceCost.note: Engineering tricks are ignored unless the paper reports an end-to-end model/policy inference frequency on comparable hardware. Diffusion estimates penalize denoising steps; autoregressive estimates account for parameter scale and cached action-token generation.

generalization.note: Shown only when a Metrics-eligible paper has evidence of real-world unseen-task, OOD, novel-object/environment, zero-shot, or equivalent transfer evaluation.
